title: Narrow broad exception handlers and add logging for silent failures
analysis: All three code changes and their corresponding tests are ALREADY IMPLEMENTED and merged to main. The worktree inherits the completed state. Specifically: (1) orchestrator.py line 52 already has `except (OSError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired):` with logger.warning on line 53 — was bare `except Exception:`. (2) memory.py lines 55-57 and 69-71 already have `logger.warning('MCP response parse error', exc_info=True)` before returning {}. (3) reconciliation.py already has `logger.debug(...)` with exc_info=True in all 5 except blocks (lines 36, 79, 115, 142, 170). Tests exist: orchestrator has test_unexpected_exception_propagates (verifies narrowing by proving RuntimeError propagates), test_subprocess_failure (OSError returns []), test_subprocess_timeout_caught (TimeoutExpired returns []); memory has TestMcpToolCallLogging with caplog WARNING assertions for both parse paths; reconciliation has TestExceptionLogging with caplog DEBUG assertions for missing DB and OperationalError. The executor should verify all tests pass and mark the task done.

steps.description: Verify orchestrator exception-narrowing tests exist and pass in test_orchestrator.py: (1) test_subprocess_failure — OSError from subprocess.run is caught, returns []. (2) test_subprocess_timeout_caught — subprocess.TimeoutExpired is caught, returns []. (3) test_unexpected_exception_propagates — RuntimeError from subprocess.run is NOT caught, proves the except clause is narrowed to (OSError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired) and does not swallow unexpected errors. Run: pytest dashboard/tests/test_orchestrator.py::TestFindRunningOrchestrators -v

Verify orchestrator.py find_running_orchestrators() has narrowed exception handler: line 52 must be `except (OSError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired):` and line 53 must have `logger.warning('Failed to run ps aux', exc_info=True)`. Also verify logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) exists at module level. All already implemented — no code change needed.

Verify memory logging tests exist and pass in test_memory.py: (1) TestMcpToolCallLogging::test_non_json_body_logs_warning — HTTP 200 with HTML body triggers resp.json() failure, caplog asserts WARNING from dashboard.data.memory. (2) TestMcpToolCallLogging::test_invalid_inner_json_logs_warning — valid outer JSON but invalid inner text triggers json.loads failure, caplog asserts WARNING from dashboard.data.memory. Run: pytest dashboard/tests/test_memory.py::TestMcpToolCallLogging -v

Verify memory.py mcp_tool_call() has WARNING logging on both parse paths: (1) lines 55-57 — except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) after resp.json() logs warning and returns {}. (2) lines 69-71 — except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError) after json.loads(text) logs warning and returns {}. Both use `logger.warning('MCP response parse error', exc_info=True)`. All already implemented — no code change needed.

Verify reconciliation logging tests exist and pass in test_reconciliation.py: (1) TestExceptionLogging::test_missing_db_logs_debug — get_recent_runs with non-existent DB path, caplog asserts DEBUG from dashboard.data.reconciliation. (2) TestExceptionLogging::test_operational_error_logs_debug — get_watermarks with empty (no-tables) DB, caplog asserts DEBUG from dashboard.data.reconciliation on OperationalError. Run: pytest dashboard/tests/test_reconciliation.py::TestExceptionLogging -v

Verify reconciliation.py has DEBUG logging in all 5 except blocks: get_recent_runs (line 36), get_watermarks (line 79), get_buffer_stats (line 115), get_burst_state (line 142), get_latest_verdict (line 170). Each uses `logger.debug('<func_name>: DB unavailable at %s', db_path, exc_info=True)`. Also verify logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) exists at module level. All already implemented — no code change needed.

design_decisions.decision: All code changes and tests are already merged to main — plan is verification-only
design_decisions.rationale: Memory context confirms 'Tasks 35, 38, 40, 41 are currently pending in Taskmaster but confirmed implemented and merged'. The worktree was created from main which already contains all changes. The executor should verify tests pass and mark the task done.

design_decisions.decision: Orchestrator uses (OSError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired) not broader exception
design_decisions.rationale: Bare `except Exception:` also catches KeyboardInterrupt (via BaseException subclass path in some contexts), SystemExit, and programming bugs (RuntimeError, TypeError, AttributeError). Narrowing to specific subprocess failure modes ensures real bugs propagate visibly. Test test_unexpected_exception_propagates proves RuntimeError is no longer swallowed.

design_decisions.decision: Memory parse errors log at WARNING, reconciliation DB errors log at DEBUG
design_decisions.rationale: MCP parse errors indicate server-side issues or proxy misconfiguration worth investigating (WARNING). Missing/locked SQLite DB is expected during startup or when reconciliation hasn't run yet (DEBUG) — would be noise at WARNING level.

Attaches the full traceback to the log record, making it possible to diagnose root cause from logs alone without reproducing the error. This is critical for production debugging — the original problem this task was filed to solve.

design_decisions.decision: Behavior unchanged — still return defaults on error
design_decisions.rationale: The task explicitly requires 'not change behavior (still return defaults) but make failures visible in logs'. Functions continue to return [], {}, or None on error. The only change is that errors are now logged instead of silently swallowed.

reuse.how: Used with `caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING/DEBUG, logger='...')` to capture and assert log records from specific loggers. Checks both level (levelno) and logger name.
Provides deterministic HTTP responses without network I/O, including simulating non-JSON bodies (HTML from reverse proxy) that trigger parse failures.
Returns path to non-existent DB file, triggering FileNotFoundError in aiosqlite.connect with URI mode.
All three files already had `import logging` and `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` at module level. The logging calls just needed to be added to the except blocks.
